title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
text: Understanding causal influences combine interact essential temporal spatial systems. Synergistic–Unique–Redundant Decomposition (SURD) framework provides principled way break total information flow variables : Unique contributions — information uniquely provided one driver; Redundant contributions — information shared multiple drivers; Synergistic contributions — information revealed drivers considered together. vignette demonstrates perform SURD analysis using infoxtr package. explore temporal spatial cases, showing infoxtr unifies time-series spatial cross-sectional causal analysis via consistent interfaces data.frame, sf, SpatRaster objects.

title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
text: simplify visualization, plotting function provided , produces SURD decomposition plot — grouped bar chart unique (U), synergistic (S), redundant (R) components, along side panel showing information leak.
code: utils_plot_surd = \(surd_list,threshold = 0,style = "shallow") {   df = surd_list |>      tibble::as_tibble() |>      dplyr::filter(vars != "InfoLeak")     if(threshold > 0){     df = dplyr::filter(df, values > threshold)   }    df = df |>      dplyr::mutate(       var_nums = stringr::str_extract_all(vars, "(?<=V)\\d+"),       nums_joined = sapply(var_nums, \(x) paste(x, collapse = '*","*')),       vars_formatted = paste0(types, "[", nums_joined, "]"),       type_order = dplyr::case_when(         types == "U" ~ 1,           types == "S" ~ 2,          types == "R" ~ 3        ),       var_count = lengths(var_nums)      )  |>      dplyr::arrange(type_order, var_count) |>      dplyr::mutate(vars = factor(vars_formatted, levels = unique(vars_formatted)))    if (style == "shallow") {     colors = c(U = "#ec9e9e", S = "#fac58c", R = "#668392", InfoLeak = "#7f7f7f")   } else {     colors = c(U = "#d62828", S = "#f77f00", R = "#003049", InfoLeak = "gray")   }    p1 = ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(x = vars, y = values, fill = types)) +     ggplot2::geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.15, show.legend = FALSE) +     ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(name = NULL, values = colors) +     ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(name = NULL, labels = function(x) parse(text = x)) +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(name = NULL, limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0),                                 breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),                                 labels = c("0", "0.25", "0.5", "0.75", "1") ) +     ggplot2::theme_bw() +     ggplot2::theme(       axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),       axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),       panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank(),       panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 2)     )    df_leak = dplyr::slice_tail(tibble::as_tibble(surd_list), n = 1)   p2 = ggplot2::ggplot(df_leak, ggplot2::aes(x = vars, y = values)) +     ggplot2::geom_col(fill = colors[4], color = "black", linewidth = 0.15) +     ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(name = NULL, expand = c(0, 0)) +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(name = NULL, limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0),                                 breaks = c(0,1), labels = c(0,1) ) +     ggplot2::theme_bw() +     ggplot2::theme(       axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),       panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank(),       panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 2)     )    patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, ncol = 2, widths = c(10,1)) }

title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
text: following sections demonstrate SURD decomposition different contexts, illustrating flexibility across temporal spatial causal analyses.

what: Air Pollution and Cardiovascular Health in Hong Kong
title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
text: investigate causal influences air pollutants incidence cardiovascular diseases, performed SURD analysis using time lag step 3 10 discretization bins. SURD results shown figure :
code: cvd = readr::read_csv(system.file("case/cvd.csv",package = "tEDM")) head(cvd) ## # A tibble: 6 × 5 ##     cvd   rsp   no2   so2    o3 ##   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> ## 1   214  73.7  74.5  19.1 17.4  ## 2   203  77.6  80.9  18.8 39.4  ## 3   202  64.8  67.1  13.8 56.4  ## 4   182  68.8  74.7  30.8  5.6  ## 5   181  49.4  62.3  23.1  3.6  ## 6   129  67.4  63.6  17.4  6.73 cvd_long = cvd |>   tibble::rowid_to_column("id") |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -id,                       names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")  fig_cvds_ts = ggplot2::ggplot(cvd_long, ggplot2::aes(x = id, y = value, color = variable)) +   ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Days (from 1995-3 to 1997-11)", y = "Concentrations or \nNO. of CVD admissions", color = "") +   ggplot2::theme_bw() +   ggplot2::theme(legend.direction = "horizontal",                  legend.position = "inside",                  legend.justification = c("center","top"),                  legend.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA)) fig_cvds_ts res_cvds = infoxtr::surd(cvd, 1, 2:5,                          lag = 15, bin = 10, threads = 6) tibble::as_tibble(res_cvds) ## # A tibble: 20 × 3 ##    vars        types      values ##    <chr>       <chr>       <dbl> ##  1 V1          U        0.00219  ##  2 V2          U        0.0155   ##  3 V3          U        0.00252  ##  4 V1_V2       R        0.00345  ##  5 V1_V4       R        0.000711 ##  6 V1_V2_V3    R        0.00506  ##  7 V1_V2_V4    R        0.00991  ##  8 V1_V2_V3_V4 R        0.0269   ##  9 V1_V2       S        0.0205   ## 10 V1_V3       S        0.0224   ## 11 V1_V4       S        0.0451   ## 12 V2_V3       S        0.0256   ## 13 V2_V4       S        0.0352   ## 14 V3_V4       S        0.0366   ## 15 V1_V2_V3    S        0.0350   ## 16 V1_V2_V4    S        0.104    ## 17 V1_V3_V4    S        0.151    ## 18 V2_V3_V4    S        0.0467   ## 19 V1_V2_V3_V4 S        0.404    ## 20 InfoLeak    InfoLeak 0.535 utils_plot_surd(res_cvds)

what: Population Density and Its Drivers in Mainland China
title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
code: popd_nb = spdep::read.gal(system.file("case/popd_nb.gal",package = "spEDM")) ## Warning in spdep::read.gal(system.file("case/popd_nb.gal", package = "spEDM")): neighbour object ## has 4 sub-graphs popd = readr::read_csv(system.file("case/popd.csv",package = "spEDM")) popd_sf = sf::st_as_sf(popd, coords = c("lon","lat"), crs = 4326) popd_sf ## Simple feature collection with 2806 features and 5 fields ## Geometry type: POINT ## Dimension:     XY ## Bounding box:  xmin: 74.9055 ymin: 18.2698 xmax: 134.269 ymax: 52.9346 ## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 ## # A tibble: 2,806 × 6 ##     popd  elev   tem   pre slope          geometry ##  * <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>       <POINT [°]> ##  1  780.     8  17.4 1528. 0.452 (116.912 30.4879) ##  2  395.    48  17.2 1487. 0.842 (116.755 30.5877) ##  3  261.    49  16.0 1456. 3.56  (116.541 30.7548) ##  4  258.    23  17.4 1555. 0.932  (116.241 30.104) ##  5  211.   101  16.3 1494. 3.34   (116.173 30.495) ##  6  386.    10  16.6 1382. 1.65  (116.935 30.9839) ##  7  350.    23  17.5 1569. 0.346 (116.677 30.2412) ##  8  470.    22  17.1 1493. 1.88  (117.066 30.6514) ##  9 1226.    11  17.4 1526. 0.208 (117.171 30.5558) ## 10  137.   598  13.9 1458. 5.92  (116.208 30.8983) ## # ℹ 2,796 more rows res_popd = infoxtr::surd(popd_sf, 1, 2:5,                          lag = 3, bin = 10, nb = popd_nb, threads = 6) tibble::as_tibble(res_popd) ## # A tibble: 19 × 3 ##    vars        types     values ##    <chr>       <chr>      <dbl> ##  1 V1          U        0.00453 ##  2 V3          U        0.0390  ##  3 V4          U        0.00349 ##  4 V1_V3       R        0.00872 ##  5 V3_V4       R        0.00269 ##  6 V1_V2_V3    R        0.00546 ##  7 V1_V2_V3_V4 R        0.110   ##  8 V1_V2       S        0.0361  ##  9 V1_V3       S        0.0461  ## 10 V1_V4       S        0.0866  ## 11 V2_V3       S        0.0458  ## 12 V2_V4       S        0.0749  ## 13 V3_V4       S        0.0858  ## 14 V1_V2_V3    S        0.0376  ## 15 V1_V2_V4    S        0.0584  ## 16 V1_V3_V4    S        0.0453  ## 17 V2_V3_V4    S        0.154   ## 18 V1_V2_V3_V4 S        0.107   ## 19 InfoLeak    InfoLeak 0.642 utils_plot_surd(res_popd)

what: Influence of Climatic and Topographic Factors on Net Primary Productivity (NPP) in Mainland China
title: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant Decomposition of Causality (SURD)
text: 🧩 See also: ?infoxtr::surd() function details. spEDM package spatial empirical dynamic modeling. tEDM package temporal empirical dynamic modeling.
code: npp = terra::rast(system.file("case/npp.tif", package = "spEDM")) npp ## class       : SpatRaster  ## size        : 404, 483, 5  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) ## resolution  : 10000, 10000  (x, y) ## extent      : -2625763, 2204237, 1877078, 5917078  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) ## coord. ref. : CGCS2000_Albers  ## source      : npp.tif  ## names       :      npp,        pre,      tem,      elev,         hfp  ## min values  :   164.00,   384.3409, -47.8194, -122.2004,  0.03390418  ## max values  : 16606.33, 23878.3555, 263.6938, 5350.4902, 44.90312195 res_npp = infoxtr::surd(npp,1, 2:5,                         lag = 2, bin = 10, threads = 6) tibble::as_tibble(res_npp) ## # A tibble: 22 × 3 ##    vars        types    values ##    <chr>       <chr>     <dbl> ##  1 V1          U     0.0777    ##  2 V2          U     0.000234  ##  3 V3          U     0.0000114 ##  4 V4          U     0.0164    ##  5 V1_V2       R     0.0948    ##  6 V1_V3       R     0.000828  ##  7 V1_V4       R     0.00514   ##  8 V1_V2_V3    R     0.0137    ##  9 V1_V2_V4    R     0.0149    ## 10 V1_V2_V3_V4 R     0.565     ## # ℹ 12 more rows utils_plot_surd(res_npp)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wenbo Lyu. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lyu W (2026). infoxtr: Information-Theoretic Measures Revealing Variable Interactions. R package version 0.1, https://stscl.github.io/infoxtr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {infoxtr: Information-Theoretic Measures for Revealing Variable Interactions},   author = {Wenbo Lyu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1},   url = {https://stscl.github.io/infoxtr/}, }

what: infoxtr | Information-Theoretic Measures for Revealing Variable Interactions
title: infoxtr | Information-Theoretic Measures for Revealing Variable Interactions
text: Information-Theoretic Measures Revealing Variable Interactions infoxtr R package analyzing variable interactions using information-theoretic measures. Originally tailored time series, methods extend seamlessly spatial cross-sectional data. Powered pure C++ engine lightweight R interface, package also exposes headers direct integration R packages. Refer package documentation https://stscl.github.io/infoxtr/ detailed information.

title: infoxtr | Information-Theoretic Measures for Revealing Variable Interactions
text: Install CRAN : Install binary version R-universe : Install source code GitHub :
code: install.packages("infoxtr", dep = TRUE) install.packages("infoxtr",                  repos = c("https://stscl.r-universe.dev",                            "https://cloud.r-project.org"),                  dep = TRUE) if (!requireNamespace("devtools")) {     install.packages("devtools") } devtools::install_github("stscl/infoxtr",                          build_vignettes = TRUE,                          dep = TRUE)

title: Conditional Entropy — ce
text: Estimate conditional entropy target variables given conditioning variables.

title: Conditional Entropy — ce
code: ce(data, target, conds, base = exp(1), type = c("cont", "disc"), k = 3)

title: Conditional Entropy — ce
text: data Observation data. target Integer vector column indices target variables. conds Integer vector column indices conditioning variables. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc".

title: Conditional Entropy — ce
text: numerical value.

title: Conditional Mutual Information — cmi
text: Estimate conditional mutual information target interacting variables given conditioning variables.

title: Conditional Mutual Information — cmi
code: cmi(   data,   target,   interact,   conds,   base = exp(1),   type = c("cont", "disc"),   k = 3,   normalize = FALSE )

title: Conditional Mutual Information — cmi
text: data Observation data. target Integer vector column indices target variables. interact Integer vector column indices interacting variables. conds Integer vector column indices conditioning variables. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc". normalize (optional) Logical; TRUE, return normalized mutual information.

title: Conditional Mutual Information — cmi
text: numerical value.

title: Conditional Mutual Information — cmi
code: set.seed(42) infoxtr::cmi(matrix(stats::rnorm(99,1,10),ncol=3),1,2,3) #> [1] 0.02440107

title: Discretization — discretize
text: Discretize numeric vector categorical classes using several commonly used discretization methods. Missing values (NA/NaN) ignored returned class 0.

title: Discretization — discretize
code: discretize(   x,   n = 5,   method = "natural",   large = 3000,   prop = 0.15,   seed = 123456789,   thr = 0.4,   iter = 100,   bps = NULL,   right_closed = TRUE )

title: Discretization — discretize
text: x vector. n (optional) Number classes. method (optional) Discretization method. One "sd", "equal", "geometric", "quantile", "manual", "natural("jenks")", "headtail"("headtails"). large (optional) Threshold sample size natural breaks sampling. prop (optional) Sampling proportion used method = "natural" input size exceeds large. seed (optional) Random seed used sampling natural breaks. thr (optional) Threshold used head/tail breaks algorithm. iter (optional) Maximum number iterations head/tail breaks. bps (optional) Numeric vector manual breakpoints used method = "manual". right_closed (optional) Logical. TRUE, intervals right-closed (e.g., (, b]). FALSE, intervals left-closed [, b).

title: Discretization — discretize
text: discretized integer vector.

title: Discretization — discretize
text: x numeric, converted integer categories via .factor().

title: Shannon Entropy — entropy
text: Estimate entropy vector using either category counts (discrete data) k-nearest neighbor estimator (continuous data).

title: Shannon Entropy — entropy
code: entropy(vec, base = exp(1), type = c("cont", "disc"), k = 3)

title: Shannon Entropy — entropy
text: vec vector. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc".

title: Shannon Entropy — entropy
text: numerical value.

title: Shannon Entropy — entropy
code: set.seed(42) infoxtr::entropy(stats::rnorm(100), type = "cont") #> [1] 1.463547 infoxtr::entropy(sample(letters[1:5], 100, TRUE), base = 2, type = "disc") #> [1] 2.298349

title: Joint Entropy — je
text: Estimate joint entropy selected variables.

title: Joint Entropy — je
code: je(data, indices, base = exp(1), type = c("cont", "disc"), k = 3)

title: Joint Entropy — je
text: data Observation data. indices Integer vector column indices include joint entropy calculation. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc".

title: Joint Entropy — je
text: numerical value.

title: Mutual Information — mi
text: Estimate mutual information target interacting variables.

title: Mutual Information — mi
code: mi(   data,   target,   interact,   base = exp(1),   type = c("cont", "disc"),   k = 3,   normalize = FALSE )

title: Mutual Information — mi
text: data Observation data. target Integer vector column indices target variables. interact Integer vector column indices interacting variables. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc". normalize (optional) Logical; TRUE, return normalized mutual information.

title: Mutual Information — mi
text: numerical value.

title: SURD — surd
text: Synergistic-Unique-Redundant decomposition causality

title: SURD — surd
code: # S4 method for class 'data.frame' surd(   data,   target,   agent,   lag = 1,   bin = 5,   method = "equal",   max.combs = 10,   threads = 1,   base = 2,   normalize = TRUE )  # S4 method for class 'sf' surd(   data,   target,   agent,   lag = 1,   bin = 5,   method = "equal",   max.combs = 10,   threads = 1,   base = 2,   normalize = TRUE,   nb = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'SpatRaster' surd(   data,   target,   agent,   lag = 1,   bin = 5,   method = "equal",   max.combs = 10,   threads = 1,   base = 2,   normalize = TRUE )

title: SURD — surd
text: data Observation data. target Integer vector column indices target variables. agent Integer vector column indices source (agent) variables. lag (optional) Lag agent variables. bin (optional) Number discretization bins. method (optional) Discretization method. One "sd", "equal", "geometric", "quantile", "natural("jenks")", "headtail"("headtails"). max.combs (optional) Maximum combination order. threads (optional) Number threads used. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. normalize (optional) Logical; TRUE, return normalized mutual information. nb (optional) Neighbours list.

title: SURD — surd
text: list. vars Character vector indicating variable combination associated information component. types Character vector indicating information type component. values Numeric vector giving magnitude information component.

title: SURD — surd
text: SURD support numeric data.

title: SURD — surd
text: Martinez-Sanchez, ., Arranz, G. & Lozano-Duran, . Decomposing causality synergistic, unique, redundant components. Nat Commun 15, 9296 (2024).

title: SURD — surd
code: columbus = sf::read_sf(system.file("case/columbus.gpkg", package="spEDM")) infoxtr::surd(columbus, 1, 2:3) #> $vars #> [1] "V1"       "V2"       "V1_V2"    "V1_V2"    "InfoLeak" #>  #> $types #> [1] "U"        "U"        "R"        "S"        "InfoLeak" #>  #> $values #> [1] 0.06337975 0.06743535 0.46246679 0.40671810 0.58577187 #>

title: Transfer Entropy — te
text: Estimate transfer entropy agent variables target variables.

title: Transfer Entropy — te
code: te(   data,   target,   agent,   lag_p = 3,   lag_q = 3,   base = exp(1),   type = c("cont", "disc"),   k = 3,   normalize = FALSE,   lag_single = FALSE )

title: Transfer Entropy — te
text: data Observation data. target Integer vector column indices target variables. agent Integer vector column indices source (agent) variables. lag_p (optional) Lag target variables. lag_q (optional) Lag agent variables. base (optional) Logarithm base entropy. Defaults exp(1) (nats). Use 2 bits 10 dits. type (optional) Estimation method: "disc" discrete entropy "cont" continuous entropy (KSG estimator). k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used continuous estimator. Ignored type = "disc". normalize (optional) Logical; TRUE, return normalized mutual information. lag_single (optional) Logical; FALSE, use full lag embedding.

title: Transfer Entropy — te
text: numerical value.

title: Transfer Entropy — te
code: set.seed(42) infoxtr::te(matrix(stats::rnorm(100,1,10),ncol=2),1,2) #> [1] 0.01700429
